char.name: Terah
char.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

friends.name: Eliezer of Damascus
friends.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.memorial_for: Terah, who set out for a land he would not reach
posts.body: My father Terah has died, here in Haran, two hundred and five years old. He led us out of Ur and pointed us toward Canaan, and he brought us this far and no further. I have been sitting with that all day. A man can spend his whole life walking toward a country and lie down at last within sight of the road that leads to it. He was the one who turned our faces away from the only home we had. Whatever comes next for us, it began with him. Rest now, father.
posts.ember: Terah dies in Haran short of Canaan (Gen 11:32); the baton passes to Abram, and God is about to speak

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_048
posts.body: I am an old man and I am starting over. We are leaving Ur, the only city my family has ever known, and setting our faces toward the land of Canaan. My son @CHAR_049, his wife @CHAR_052, and my grandson @CHAR_053 come with me. I cannot fully say why. Something in me could not stay another season. We have come as far as Haran, and here, I think, these old bones will rest a while. The young ones will go on from here. They always do.
posts.ember: the line of Shem leaves Ur of the Chaldeans (Gen 11:31); the journey toward the land begins before the promise is ever spoken

author_info.name: Terah
author_info.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.
